claimText: GSAT-7 (Rukmini) is India's first officially dedicated military satellite and is utilized by the Indian Navy.
evidenceExcerpt: GSAT-7 (Rukmini) is India's first officially dedicated military satellite, used by the Navy.
examUse: Correct-pair questions matching military satellites to their respective armed forces branches.
distractorHooks: Claiming GSAT-7 is used by the Army or Air Force
Claiming it is a dual-use civilian satellite

claimText: GSAT-7A (Angry Bird) is a military satellite that helps the Indian Air Force connect platforms such as AWACS, UAVs, and aircraft.
evidenceExcerpt: GSAT-7A (Angry Bird) helps the IAF connect platforms like AWACS, UAVs, and aircraft.
examUse: Statement-based questions on the specific applications of the GSAT-7 series satellites.
distractorHooks: Attributing GSAT-7A to the Indian Navy or Army
Stating it is primarily for civilian weather monitoring

claimText: GSAT-7B is a military satellite being developed for the Indian Army to enhance surveillance and communication in border areas.
evidenceExcerpt: GSAT-7B is being developed for the Army to enhance surveillance and communication in border areas.
examUse: Correct-pair questions matching the GSAT-7B satellite to the Indian Army.
distractorHooks: Pairing GSAT-7B with the Indian Navy or Air Force
Claiming it is an anti-satellite weapon system

claimText: GSAT-7C is being developed for the Indian Air Force and includes ground hubs for real-time, secure communication.
evidenceExcerpt: GSAT-7C is being developed for the Air Force and includes ground hubs for real-time, secure communication.
examUse: Statement-based questions regarding the features and designated user of the GSAT-7C satellite.
distractorHooks: Pairing GSAT-7C with the Indian Army
Claiming it lacks ground hubs or secure communication capabilities
